title: Compressed Air System Energy Cost Calculator
description: Industrial-grade tool to calculate total energy cost of compressed air systems, identify hidden losses, and suggest efficiency improvements based on ISO 50001, Lean, and Six Sigma principles.

inputs.label: Compressor Motor Power Rating
inputs.businessContext: Nameplate power of the compressor motor. Typically from 5 kW to 5000 kW for industrial units.

inputs.label: Motor Efficiency
inputs.businessContext: Efficiency of the electric motor driving the compressor. Premium efficiency motors (IE3/IE4) are >90%.

inputs.label: Compressor Type
inputs.businessContext: Different compressor types have different specific power curves and maintenance profiles.

inputs.label: Operating Hours per Year
inputs.businessContext: Total annual operating hours. Typical industrial: 8000 hours (2 shifts, 5 days).

inputs.label: Load Factor (Average % of Full Load)
inputs.businessContext: Average percentage of full load capacity at which the compressor operates. Includes part-load and unloaded periods.

inputs.label: Electricity Cost
inputs.businessContext: Blended average cost per kWh including demand charges. Typical range $0.05-$0.30.

inputs.label: Estimated System Leakage
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of compressed air lost through leaks. Typical industrial systems: 20-30%.

inputs.label: System Pressure Setpoint
inputs.businessContext: Operating pressure at the compressor discharge. Higher pressure increases energy consumption by ~1% per 0.1 bar.

inputs.label: Average Ambient Temperature
inputs.businessContext: Average ambient temperature at compressor intake. Higher temperature reduces air density and increases power.

inputs.label: Variable Speed Drive (VSD) Installed
inputs.businessContext: VSD can reduce energy consumption by 15-35% under part-load conditions.

inputs.label: Maintenance Quality Level
inputs.businessContext: Maintenance quality affects efficiency, leakage, and pressure drop. Poor maintenance can increase energy use by 10-20%.

rules.message: With VSD, load factor above 85% may indicate oversizing. Consider fixed-speed alternative for better ROI.
High leakage combined with poor maintenance suggests urgent leak detection program.
Pressure above 10 bar(g) is uncommon for general industrial use. Verify actual requirement.

thresholds.alert: Annual energy cost exceeds $50,000. Consider energy audit and optimization.
Leakage above 30% is critical. Immediate leak repair can save up to 40% of energy.
Motor efficiency below 85%. Consider replacing with IE4 premium motor.

formulas.description: Actual electrical power input to compressor motor
Total annual energy consumption at average load
Energy lost due to system leaks
Energy penalty due to high pressure (1% per 0.1 bar above 6 bar baseline)
Efficiency improvement factor if VSD installed
Penalty for poor maintenance (ISO 50001 best practices)
formulas.formula: CASE maintenance_quality WHEN 'poor' THEN 1.15 WHEN 'standard' THEN 1.05 WHEN 'excellent' THEN 1.0 END

formulas.description: Energy consumption adjusted for pressure, temperature, VSD, and maintenance
Primary result: Total annual energy cost of compressed air system

primary.label: Total Annual Energy Cost
primary.description: Primary result: Total annual cost to operate the compressed air system including all losses and corrections.

components.label: Base Energy Cost (at reference conditions)
components.description: Cost without pressure, temperature, VSD, or maintenance adjustments.

components.label: Cost of Leakage
components.description: Annual cost attributed to compressed air leaks.

components.label: Pressure Penalty Cost
components.description: Additional cost due to operating above 6 bar(g) baseline.

components.label: Temperature Penalty Cost
components.description: Additional cost due to ambient temperature above 20°C.

components.label: Maintenance Penalty Cost
components.description: Additional cost due to suboptimal maintenance practices.

components.label: VSD Savings (if applicable)
components.description: Cost savings from variable speed drive operation.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Leakage
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Energy lost through system leaks. Typically 20-30% of total consumption.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Pressure Drop Across Filters/Dryers
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Excessive pressure drop increases compressor discharge pressure requirement.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Inadequate Maintenance
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Poor maintenance leads to higher friction, leakage, and inefficiency.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: System Oversizing
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Compressor running at low load factor indicates oversizing, wasting energy.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Heat of Compression Not Recovered
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Waste heat can be recovered for space heating or process use.

suggestedActions.description: Implement a leak detection and repair program. Target: reduce leakage to <10%.
Reduce system pressure setpoint by 1 bar if possible. Each 1 bar reduction saves ~7% energy.
Install VSD on compressor if load varies >30%. Expected savings 15-35%.
Upgrade maintenance to predictive (IoT-based). Replace filters, check belts, lubricate.
Install heat recovery system to capture waste heat for building heating or preheat.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Result
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Total annual energy cost adjusted by a confidence factor (0.8 to 1.2) based on input data quality and completeness.
